name: The Gunslinger
description: Fast hands, sharp instincts, and a blaster that never leaves your side. The Gunslinger is a pistol specialist who thrives in the chaos of close-to-mid range firefights — drawing first, shooting last, and making every Opening count.
fluff: They say Cad Bane could clear a room before anyone heard the first shot. Captain Rex would draw both DC-17s mid-roll and put rounds on target before he hit the ground. Han Solo shot Greedo under a table because he was bored of the conversation. You're not that good. Not yet.

abilities.name: Born to the Blaster
abilities.rule: Firing a Pistol while Engaged does not present an Opening. You may draw or holster Pistols as part of any Attack action at no Maneuver cost. On a successful Join Battle, you may immediately draw one or both Pistols.

abilities.name: Akimbo
abilities.rule: When dual wielding Pistols, you ignore the Control die step-down penalty. You keep the Power die step-up. Gambits from either equipped Pistol may be declared on any Pistol attack.

abilities.name: Dead Drop
abilities.rule: Ranged (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. Draw down on a target and force them to react — you don't fire, you just let them see where this is going. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you've shown your hand.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Choose one: the target must spend their next Maneuver taking Cover, OR they present an Opening to you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. They present an Opening to you. If you Exploit it this round, step up Effect by 1 Tier.

abilities.name: Fan the Hammer
abilities.rule: After resolving a Pistol attack, spend 1 tier. If your Control die result was 8+ and the target takes damage, the target immediately presents an Opening to you which you may Exploit.

abilities.name: Two for Flinching
abilities.rule: When you Exploit an Opening with a Pistol and the attack deals damage, the target presents another Opening to any ally in the zone. Your shots crack the door wide open for the whole crew.

name: The Shockboxer
description: Fists, shock-gloves, and an iron chin. The Shockboxer is a disciplined fighter who builds momentum through combinations — each hit setting up the next until the opponent can't stand anymore. You don't need a blaster. You need them close enough to hit.
fluff: Shockboxing pits across the Outer Rim draw crowds from Nar Shaddaa to Ord Mantell. The fighters wear shock-gloves that amplify impact without cutting skin — the sport is about endurance, reading your opponent, and finding the knockout. The best boxers don't just win rounds. They end fights. You trained in those pits. You learned to take a hit, give two back, and stay standing when everyone else is on the floor.

abilities.name: Iron Chin
abilities.rule: You may use Brawl (Physique) in place of Endure for Defense rolls while Engaged. When you take Stun damage and remain conscious, gain [Optimized] on your next Brawl attack.

abilities.name: Combo Counter
abilities.rule: Track Brawl hits that deal damage to the same target. On the 2nd hit: target gains [Rattled]. On the 3rd hit: target gains [Exposed]. Combo Counter resets at the end of your turn.

abilities.name: Body Work
abilities.rule: After resolving a Brawl attack that deals damage, spend 1 tier. This hit counts as 2 hits for your Combo Counter. A well-placed body shot fast-tracks the punishment.

abilities.name: Roll With It
abilities.rule: When you defend using Brawl (via Iron Chin), spend 1 tier from the defense result. The attacker immediately presents an Opening to you.

abilities.name: The Knockout
abilities.rule: When your Combo Counter reaches 3+ on a target and your Brawl attack resolves at Masterful or higher, you may declare a Knockout. The target is immediately [Incapacitated]. Once per scene.

name: The Ghost
description: Misdirection, concealment, and the art of not being where anyone expects you. The Ghost doesn't fight fair — they fight from positions the enemy didn't know existed, and when the walls close in, they get everyone out.
fluff: Intelligence operatives, resistance saboteurs, and the kind of people who walk through Imperial checkpoints without anyone remembering their face. The Ghost doesn't win fights. They win extractions. They win infiltrations. They win by making the enemy argue about whether anyone was ever really there. Cassian Andor walked into Scarif knowing he wasn't coming back. You walk into places knowing you will.

abilities.name: Gray Man
abilities.rule: When you benefit from concealment, you may use Stealth (Reflex) in place of Evasion on Dodge defense rolls. Additionally, while benefiting from concealment, you do not present Openings originating from the Engaged condition.

abilities.name: Blind Spot
abilities.rule: When you Dodge an attack from an Engaged target, spend 1 tier. Your next attack against that target before the end of your next turn gains [Optimized].

abilities.name: Slip
abilities.rule: Stealth (Reflex) vs target's Resolve. Use movement and misdirection to fade from a target's focus — a feint, a peripheral slide, blending into the chaos. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you tried to be clever and they saw right through it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Immediate.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Tactical (until start of your next turn).

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: You gain concealment from the target. Duration: Tactical. Immediately move 1 zone.

abilities.name: Vanishing Act
abilities.rule: When you Dodge and your Control die is 8+, spend your Exploit pip. You gain concealment from the attacker. Duration: Tactical.

abilities.name: Exit Strategy
abilities.rule: Once per Act, if all allies and yourself have concealment and none are restrained or held in place, you may declare an Opening for all allies and yourself explicitly to escape the scene. There must be a plausible means of egress, even if unconventional — a grate, a shaft, a window, a crowd. The GM narrates the escape.

name: The Ichor Witch
description: Ichor flows outward — into wounds, into objects, into the dead. The Ichor Witch channels the magick of Dathomir through physical talismans, sympathetic bonds, and sheer force of will.
fluff: The Nightsisters of Dathomir learned long ago that the Force is not invisible. It has color. It has weight. It has teeth. They were right.

abilities.name: Thread of Ichor
abilities.rule: Your Force expression manifests as visible green ichor energy — unmistakably magick, never subtle. Targets who witness your ichor magick for the first time in a scene gain [Rattled]. You may use Alter (Presence) in place of Medicine (Wits) when performing Treat Injury — you channel ichor into wounds rather than applying conventional medicine.

abilities.name: Ichor Talisman
abilities.rule:
**Creation**: Spend 1 Edge Point during a Narrative scene to imbue a portable object with ichor, creating a Force talisman. One active talisman at a time (until T3). The talisman must be willingly accepted by the bearer — it cannot be planted or hidden on an unwitting target. If not actively carried by the bearer, the talisman is inert. The talisman contains a portion of your Force signature — any Force-sensitive who examines it may identify its creator. In the wrong hands, it can be used to target you.

**Attunement**: When creating the talisman, choose a discipline. The object should reflect the discipline's nature (a weapon for Melee or Ranged, a medpac for Medicine, a lockpick set for Skulduggery, a focus crystal for a Spark discipline, etc. — GM adjudicates). That discipline becomes a favored discipline for the bearer while carried.

**Sense Through the Bond**: While the bearer carries your talisman, you may spend an Action to Assess using Sense (The Spark) through the bearer's senses — no line of sight required. This establishes a sympathetic connection for the remainder of the scene.

**While the connection is active**:
- **Ichor Surge** — Spend 1 of your own Edge Points (no action cost) to trigger an Edge spend for the talisman bearer. This functions as if the bearer spent their own Edge — Tier Boost on their favored disciplines, Unleashed effects, vocation abilities.
- **Pervert the Bond** — Channel the dark side through the connection to use Arise (T4) or True Possession (T5) on or through the bearer — no line of sight required. This immediately flips one Hope to Toll. The talisman blackens and goes inert.

**No connection, no channel.** If you haven't established the Sense link this scene, the talisman is just a charm (the favored discipline still applies passively). Ichor Surge and Pervert the Bond require the active connection.

abilities.name: Ichor Infusion
abilities.rule:
**Two Talismans**: You may now have 2 active Ichor Talismans simultaneously. Each may be attuned to a different discipline.

**Weapon Infusion**: When the talisman is imbued into a weapon, it retains all T2 talisman properties (attunement, favored discipline, Sense connection, Ichor Surge, Pervert the Bond). Additionally, the weapon gains an ichor-infused quality — attacks with the weapon unlock the Blight gambit.

gambits.name: Blight
gambits.rule: When the bearer hits with an ichor-infused weapon and deals damage, spend 1 tier. The target gains [Rattled]. If the target is already [Rattled], they gain [Exposed] instead.

abilities.name: Arise
abilities.rule:
Alter (Presence). Power = Roll. You channel ichor into fallen creatures or corpses in your zone, animating them as thralls. Corpses must be physically present in the fiction.

Directing Thralls: Use the Coordinate maneuver, substituting Control (Grit) for Tactics (Wits). If you are [Incapacitated], all thralls collapse.
abilities.risk: On failure, the ichor dissipates — the dead do not answer. Wasted Action.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Animate 1 corpse as a simple thrall. Acts on your initiative. Collapses at end of round.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Animate up to 2 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Animate up to 3 corpses as thralls. Act on your initiative, last until end of scene. GM may grant them one echo ability from their living stat block.

abilities.name: True Possession
abilities.rule:
Alter (Presence) vs target's Resolve. You fully project your spirit into the target's body. Your own body goes [Helpless] for the duration. Line of sight required (unless using Pervert the Bond through an Ichor Talisman).

Sustain: 1 Edge Point per round to maintain. If you can't pay, possession ends immediately — you snap back to your body [Disoriented].
abilities.risk: On failure, the target's mind lashes back — you gain [Rattled] + [Disoriented] until end of round. The target knows someone tried to get inside their head.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The host spirit fights hard. All actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] + [Weakened]. Sustaining also costs your Maneuver each round in addition to 1 Edge.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: The spirit resists but you have the upper hand. Actions through the possessed body suffer [Disoriented] only. Costs 1 Edge per round to sustain.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Total override. Full actions, no penalties. The host spirit is suppressed entirely. Still costs 1 Edge per round. Target retains no memory of what happened during possession.

name: The Noble
description: Words are weapons, presence is armor, and the right phrase at the right moment can topple empires. The Noble commands rooms, reads people like open books, and bends wills without ever raising a hand — or a voice.
fluff: Bail Organa held the Senate floor while a star system burned. Mon Mothma whispered three sentences and built a rebellion. Padmé Amidala walked into a war zone in a white dress and made a king kneel. You're not royalty — but when you speak, people listen.

abilities.name: Commanding Presence
abilities.rule: Charm replaces Intimidate for any action where you would normally use Intimidate. You project authority through force of personality rather than threat of violence. When another ability or effect calls for an Intimidate roll, you may use Charm (Presence) instead.

abilities.name: Read the Room
abilities.rule: Study a target's body language, tone, and social cues. Roll Charm (Presence) — no defense, this is an Assess-style read. You learn what makes them tick and how to use it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn one useful detail about the target's emotional state, motivation, or social leverage.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus choose one: gain +1 tier on your next social action against this target, OR your next social action against them is [Optimized].

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Learn the above. You gain both benefits — +1 tier AND [Optimized] on your next social action against the target.

abilities.name: Compel
abilities.rule: Make a demand, issue a directive, or simply make it clear what you expect. Roll Charm (Presence) vs target's Resolve. Pure social pressure — no threats, no lies, just the weight of your personality.
abilities.risk: On failure, the target sees through the play — they are immune to Compel from you for the rest of the scene, and the GM may shift their attitude one step hostile.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target's next interaction with you or your crew is [Disoriented] — they're off-balance, second-guessing.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target must either comply with a reasonable request OR publicly justify their refusal. GM reveals one thing the target is trying to protect or hide.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target complies with your request. Their attitude toward you shifts one step favorable for the rest of the scene.

abilities.name: Entourage
abilities.rule:
When you arrive in a social scene, you may declare one willing NPC present as part of your Entourage. That NPC will carry out reasonable non-combat requests for you for the duration of the scene — run errands, make introductions, hold a position, relay messages.

If you attempt a social action while your Entourage member is visibly supporting you (backing your play, standing at your shoulder), that action gains [Optimized].

Limit: one Entourage member per scene. The GM can rule an NPC unwilling if there's a strong narrative reason.

abilities.name: Voice of Authority
abilities.rule:
Upgrades Compel.

**Address the Room:** Your Compel maneuver can now target a group of individuals rather than a single target. Roll once — all targets defend with their own Resolve individually, but the effect tier is determined by your single Control result.

**Sustained Influence:** When you score a Masterful Success (8+ on Control), instead of increasing the effect tier by one level, you may choose to extend the duration of your Compel through the end of the Act. This does not guarantee compliance — but so long as you maintain your charming approach and targets are not endangered, their disposition toward you remains at the level your Compel established. Break the charm (threaten them, put them in danger) and it snaps immediately.

name: The Investigator
description: Notices what others miss, connects what others can't, and arrives at the truth before anyone else knows there's a question. The Investigator doesn't fight harder or talk smoother — they simply know more, see more, and understand the significance of what they find.
fluff: Obi-Wan traced a single saberdart through a deleted archive to uncover a secret clone army. Cassian Andor survived fifteen years of intelligence work by reading rooms faster than anyone in them. Thrawn studied a species' art and reverse-engineered their entire battle doctrine. You've been doing this long enough that the galaxy stopped surprising you — now it just confirms what you already suspected.

abilities.name: Trained Eye
abilities.rule:
You may use Investigation in place of Insight when attempting to read an NPC target — you read people through observation and evidence rather than empathy. You notice the inconsistency in their story, not the tremor in their voice.

Additionally, even on a failed Assess, you still learn one detail related to the assessment target. You never walk away completely empty-handed.

abilities.name: Connect the Dots
abilities.rule:
When you succeed on an Assess, you may spend one of your earned questions to instead make a **declaration**: state a connection between two facts, people, or events you've encountered this session. The GM must confirm if you're correct, partially correct, or wrong — and if partially correct, they adjust your declaration to the truth rather than simply saying no.

This isn't asking the GM for information — it's proposing a theory and forcing the narrative to respond.

abilities.name: Keen Insight
abilities.rule:
Dedicate your full attention to a single person, object, or location that the GM confirms is narratively significant — a crime scene, a key NPC, a suspicious artifact, a location tied to an active lead. If the target holds no relevant information, the GM may simply say "Nothing to see here" and the maneuver is not spent.

This goes deeper than Assess — you're not asking questions, you're reconstructing what happened.
abilities.risk: Tunnel vision. You are fully absorbed — you cannot act on or respond to anything else happening in the scene until your next beat. If the situation shifts around you, you miss it entirely.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Learn the most recent significant event involving the subject — who was here, what they did, approximately when.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Learn the above, plus the GM reveals a hidden detail — something concealed, altered, or deliberately obscured. A false trail, a removed object, a covered wound.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Full reconstruction. The GM narrates a brief flashback of what happened at this location or to this subject. You see it play out. The crew may act on this as reliable intelligence.

abilities.name: Follow the Thread
abilities.rule:
Upgrades Keen Insight.

When you score a Masterful Success (8+ on Control) on Keen Insight, after resolving the reconstruction, you may immediately make an Assess maneuver at no additional action cost. This follow-up Assess gains +1 Tier to its effect result.

Your questions on this Assess must pertain directly to details revealed by the Keen Insight reconstruction — you're drilling into what you just saw, not fishing for new information.

abilities.name: The Confrontation
abilities.rule:
When you confront the target of your investigation — the person, group, or entity your Keen Insight and Assess results have been building toward — you gain [Optimized] on all Assess and social actions directed at them. You know their patterns, their tells, their pressure points. You've done the work.

If the confrontation turns hostile, you may spend 1 Edge Point. All allies gain [Optimized] and [Empowered] on their Join Battle rolls. You saw this coming. You briefed the crew. Nobody's caught flat-footed.

name: The Seer
description: The Force is a river of information — past, present, and the faintest currents of what may come. The Seer drinks from that river. They perceive what others cannot: the fracture lines in people and events, the split-second warnings that turn impossible dodges into casual sidesteps, and the shatterpoints where everything can break.
fluff: Quinlan Vos touched a wall and watched a murder happen three days after the blood dried. Cal Kestis picked up a fallen lightsaber and lived its owner’s last stand. Mace Windu saw the fracture in the Confederacy before anyone else knew it existed. You’re not that powerful. Not yet. But when the Force shows you the crack in everything — pay attention.

abilities.name: Combat Sense
abilities.rule: You may use Sense (Wits) in place of Tactics (Wits) for Join Battle rolls. The Force whispers the shape of violence before it begins. Additionally, on a Masterful Success (Control 8+) on Join Battle, choose one: gain +1 Effect Tier on your initiative result, OR gain [Optimized] on all Attack and Defense rolls until the end of your first Action.

abilities.name: Force Sight
abilities.rule: You may use Sense (Wits) in place of any discipline normally called for on Assess actions. Your perception extends beyond physical senses — you ignore darkness, cover, concealment, and solid barriers when assessing. You detect life presences, emotional states, and Force signatures. This does not replace Sense discipline gambits — those still function normally on top of this substitution. The GM adjudicates effective range based on the fiction.

abilities.name: Shatterpoint Sense
abilities.rule: Perceive the connections between people, events, and objects through the Force. Roll Sense (Wits), no defense. You do not learn facts — you perceive relationships, emotional weight, and the web of causality that binds things together.
abilities.risk: On failure, you gain [Disoriented]. Particularly traumatic echoes may add [Rattled] at the GM’s discretion.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: A vague sense that a connection exists between two elements in the scene. You know something links them, but not the nature or significance.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: You perceive the nature and emotional weight of the connection. The GM reveals the relationship’s character — loyalty, fear, debt, hatred, love, obligation. You may ask 1 question about the connection.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: The full web of connections unfolds. You see how multiple elements relate — the chain of causality, the pressure points, the fault lines. The GM reveals the key pressure point in the web. You may ask 2 questions.

abilities.name: Precognitive Counter
abilities.rule: When you Endure or Dodge and your Control die is 8+, spend your Exploit pip. Instead of the standard +1 Tier bonus from Masterful Success, the attacker presents an Opening to you. You saw the strike before it landed and positioned yourself to punish it.

abilities.name: Shatterpoint Strike
abilities.rule: Once per scene. Roll Sense (Wits) vs target’s Resolve. You perceive the shatterpoint — the exact fracture line in the target’s defense, stance, or resolve. You must attack the target before the end of the round or the moment passes. If the attack is not made, the shatterpoint closes and the maneuver is wasted.
abilities.risk: On failure, the maneuver is wasted and the target knows you tried to read them — they sense the probe.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed] and [Optimized] applies to your next attack against them this round.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Exposed]. Your next attack against them this round gains [Optimized] and [Empowered].

name: The Altus Sopor
description: The ancient practice of deepening one’s focus on the Force through mastery of the body and breath. Altus Sopor encompasses the Art of Movement, Force Speed, and the meditative void of Emptiness — the foundational Control disciplines that allow a practitioner to channel the Force inward and become something more than their physical form allows.
fluff: Altus Sopor was the traditional term for the ability to increase a user’s focus on the Force. It incorporated alchaka, breath control, Force speed, and the meditative void — the foundation of every Jedi’s control training. Masters who perfected the sopor could merge so deeply into the Force that they became nearly invisible to others. You have begun walking that path.

abilities.name: Art of Movement
abilities.rule: You may use Control (Grit) in place of Evasion (Reflex) for all Dodge defense rolls — you evade through Force-guided movement: dodging traps, debris, hazards, and attacks with preternatural grace. You gain narrative permission for wall-running, Force-assisted leaps, and vertical traversal through the Force. This ability is DISABLED while you are affected by [Rattled], [Disoriented], or any condition that disrupts mental focus.

abilities.name: Enhance Attribute
abilities.rule: Diceless Maneuver. Spend 1 Edge + your Maneuver pip. Choose one arena: Physique or Reflex. That arena gains +1 Effect Tier on all rolls. Duration: Tactical. At the start of each subsequent turn, you may spend 1 Edge to sustain the enhancement — no action cost. You may only enhance one arena at a time. Switching arenas requires a new activation.

abilities.name: Force Speed
abilities.rule: Channel the Force into explosive velocity. Roll Control (Grit). The result generates Move Points that must be spent on your Move action this turn (1 point = 1 zone). Movement fueled by Force Speed presents NO Openings, even when leaving Engaged zones. Unspent points are lost at the end of your turn. Force Speed movement fuels Art of Movement traversal (wall-running, vertical movement).
abilities.risk: Failure wastes the maneuver — the Force does not answer your call for speed this round.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: 2 Move Points.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: 4 Move Points.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: 6 Move Points.

abilities.name: Flowing Counter
abilities.rule: When you Dodge against an Engaged attacker and achieve a Masterful Success (Control 8+), instead of the standard +1 Tier bonus, the attacker presents an Opening to you which you may immediately Exploit (costs your Exploit pip). Your evasion flows seamlessly into a counterattack position.

abilities.name: Emptiness
abilities.rule: Short Rest only. Roll Control (Grit). The current Toll count on the Destiny tracker sets the static difficulty. While meditating (narrative truth, always active during the rest): you are undetectable by Force senses, you clear 1 mental condition ([Rattled] or [Disoriented]), and you are physically vulnerable and interruptible. To carry effects into the next scene, spend 1 Edge.
abilities.risk: On failure, the Edge spent to carry effects forward is wasted — the meditation did not reach sufficient depth.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Force detection against you is [Weakened] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting. Additionally, you are immune to [Rattled] and [Disoriented] until the end of the next scene.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful. Additionally, gain +1 Effect Tier on all Control (Grit) rolls until the end of the next scene.

name: The Telekinetic Savant
description: Raw telekinetic power refined into mastery. The Telekinetic Savant doesn’t just move objects — they weaponize the Force itself. Kinetic Impulse, precision manipulation, and telekinetically wielded weapons are the tools of a practitioner who has turned the most fundamental Force ability into an art form.
fluff: Vader choked admirals from across a Star Destroyer. Dooku hurled Senate platforms like toys. Kreia wielded three lightsabers without touching a single one. Starkiller pulled a Star Destroyer out of orbit. You’re not there yet. But when you reach out with the Force and the room rearranges itself around your will — that’s the path you’re walking.

abilities.name: Kinetic Impulse
abilities.rule: Project the Force outward to push or pull a target through space. Roll Alter (Presence) vs target’s Defense. Choose push or pull direction on hit. This is weaponized telekinesis — directing the Force at people, not just objects.
abilities.risk: Losing control of the Force may cause unintended collateral damage.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Push or pull the target 1 zone in any direction.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Push or pull the target 2 zones. Target gains [Disoriented] Tactical.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Push or pull the target 3 zones. Target gains [Prone]. If the target collides with a solid boundary before completing their forced movement, they take 1 Vitality damage per zone of movement not completed.

abilities.name: Precision Telekinesis
abilities.rule: You gain narrative permission to perform fine, complex manipulations with the Telekinesis maneuver — tasks that would normally require hands, tools, or direct physical contact. Examples: extracting a slug from a wound, typing an access code from across the room, picking a lock mechanism, disarming a detonator, threading a cable, removing a restraint bolt. The GM still sets difficulty based on complexity, but the permission is granted — your telekinesis is a scalpel when you need it to be, not just a battering ram. This does NOT grant discipline substitution — if the task calls for Medicine or Tech, you still roll that discipline. You just don’t need to be touching the thing.

abilities.name: Kinetic Combat
abilities.rule: Telekinetically wield a weapon — it fights independently, guided by your will. Roll Alter (Presence) to activate. The weapon can target enemies in your zone or an adjacent zone. Attacks made through Kinetic Combat use Melee (Presence) — attribute swap. Sustained: spend your Maneuver each round to maintain (no additional roll). To re-roll for a better tier, you may roll again instead of sustaining — failure means the weapon drops wherever it was. If you do not spend your Maneuver to sustain, the weapon falls.
abilities.risk: Failure on activation or re-establishment means the weapon drops — you lose telekinetic control.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: The weapon can Attack as your Action using Melee (Presence). Raw strikes only — no Gambits, no Exploits. Your control is sufficient to fight, but not to finesse.

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: As Fleeting, but your form is precise enough to use any qualifying Gambits on the attack. GM discretion on edge cases requiring physical contact.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: As Masterful, plus the weapon can use Exploits. Full combat capability through pure telekinetic mastery.

abilities.name: Force Grip
abilities.rule: After rolling Kinetic Impulse, spend 1 tier. Remove all forced movement from the result. Instead, deal Vitality damage equal to the number of zones the reduced tier would have generated (Fleeting: 1, Masterful: 2, Legendary: 3). Target gains [Restrained] Tactical. The effect lasts 1 round. To maintain: spend your Action next turn and roll Kinetic Impulse again. Success continues the grip and reapplies damage. If you do not roll, the target is freed.

abilities.name: Telekinetic Mastery
abilities.rule:
You have achieved total command of telekinetic Force projection. Three benefits:

**Scale Mastery:** You ignore the first step-down penalty when using any telekinetic Force ability (Telekinesis, Kinetic Impulse, Force Grip, Kinetic Combat) against targets of larger scale. Speeder-scale targets impose no penalty. Starship-scale targets impose only 1 step-down instead of 2.

gambits.name: Split Focus
gambits.rule: When using any targeted TK ability (Telekinesis, Kinetic Impulse, Kinetic Combat, Force Grip), you may apply the effect to two separate targets. Both targets must be in the same targetable area (same zone or adjacent zone as the ability allows).

gambits.name: Force Repulse
gambits.rule: Modifies Kinetic Impulse. Range 0 (your zone only). Instead of targeting a single enemy, the impulse affects ALL targets in your zone — allies included. Everyone gets pushed. The Kinetic Impulse tier effect applies to all targets equally.

name: The Duelist
description: Precision, timing, and a blade held with intent. The Duelist is a trained close-combat specialist who controls the fight through footwork, measure, and reading their opponent — parrying, waiting, and striking when the geometry is right. Whether the weapon is a vibroblade or a lightsaber, the discipline is the same.
fluff: Makashi was a lightsaber form built for dueling other saber-wielders — precise, economical, and contemptuous of brute strength. Count Dooku used it to dismantle two Jedi at once without breaking a sweat. But the same principles show up in fencing salons on Coruscant, vibroblade pits on Nar Shaddaa, and back-alley knife fights on Corellia. A Kel Dor bodyguard with a force pike. A Mandalorian with a beskad. A dock worker who took three months of lessons and kept the blade. The weapon doesn't make the duelist. The hours do.

abilities.name: En Garde
abilities.rule: You may Endure melee attacks using Melee (Physique) in place of Endure. When you do, treat your armor rating as Light for the Physique step calculation — even if you are unarmored, your blade compensates. You may draw or ready a Melee weapon as part of any Attack action at no Maneuver cost.

abilities.name: Read the Blade
abilities.rule: After resolving an Endure defense using Melee (via En Garde), spend 1 tier. You gain [Optimized] on your next Melee weapon attack against that attacker before the end of your next turn. You read their technique in the exchange and positioned yourself for the counter.

abilities.name: Press the Advantage
abilities.rule: Melee (Physique) vs target's Resolve. Drive forward with controlled aggression — precise bladework that tests your opponent's nerve and forces them to give ground. Risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target.
abilities.risk: On failure, you present an Opening to the target — you overextended and they saw it.

effect.label: Fleeting
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled].

effect.label: Masterful
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled]. Target presents an Opening to you.

effect.label: Legendary
effect.description: Target gains [Rattled] and [Exposed]. Target presents an Opening to you.

abilities.name: Riposte
abilities.rule: When you defend using Melee (via En Garde) and score 8+ on your Control die, you may forgo the standard +1 Effect tier. Instead, the attacker immediately presents an Opening which you may Exploit to make an immediate Melee attack against them.

abilities.name: Measure of a Man
abilities.rule: Use your Maneuver to Assess an Engaged opponent using Melee (Wits) — reading their guard, footwork, and tells. You gain the normal Assess questions from the GM (as if using Tactics). Additionally, you may spend 1 Edge. If you do, your next Melee weapon attack against that target this round gains [Optimized] and [Empowered]. You've already won. They just don't know it yet.
